name: Ory Open-Source Identity Platform API
what_it_does: Open-source identity and access management platform with REST APIs — comprising Ory Kratos (user management), Ory Hydra (OAuth2/OIDC server), Ory Keto (permissions/access control), and Ory Oathkeeper (zero-trust proxy), available self-hosted or as Ory Network cloud.
use_cases: Self-hosted identity management with full control over authentication flows via API
OAuth2/OIDC server for machine-to-machine auth and third-party integrations
Fine-grained permission management with Google Zanzibar-style ACL via Ory Keto
Building custom authentication UIs backed by Ory Kratos identity management API
Zero-trust network proxy with Ory Oathkeeper for service-to-service auth
not_for: Teams needing managed SaaS without self-hosting capacity or expertise
Simple authentication without need for customization and self-hosting
Organizations without engineering capacity to operate distributed identity infrastructure
best_when: A team needs maximum control over authentication flows with open-source self-hosting, full auditability, and comprehensive identity infrastructure components.
avoid_when: You prefer fully managed auth-as-a-service without self-hosting complexity, or don't need the flexibility of open-source.

auth.notes: Ory Network uses project API key or OAuth2 tokens. Self-hosted instances use admin API endpoints (should be network-restricted). OAuth2 via Ory Hydra for third-party auth flows.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Ory Network free: 1,000 MAU, unlimited self-hosted use
pricing.paid_tiers: Developer from $29/month; Business from $229; Enterprise custom; self-hosted: free
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source — completely free to self-host with no limits. Ory Network cloud for managed hosting. Popular in enterprise for maximum control. German company with strong privacy focus.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits; Ory Network: plan-based rate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Comprehensive API documentation across all components (Kratos, Hydra, Keto, Oathkeeper). Generated SDKs from OpenAPI spec for all major languages. Each component has its own API surface — agents must understand which component to call.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Identity and permission operations are generally idempotent for same parameters. OAuth2 token issuance creates new tokens. Identity creation idempotent with same traits.
known_agent_gotchas: Four separate components (Kratos, Hydra, Keto, Oathkeeper) — steep learning curve
Admin API endpoints should be network-restricted — not public internet accessible
Self-hosted requires orchestrating multiple services — use Docker Compose or Kubernetes
UI flows (login, registration) require browser — server-side flows use different endpoints

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable. TLS for Ory Network; configurable self-hosted. OAuth2 with comprehensive scopes. GDPR compliant. German company with privacy-by-design. Security-focused team with responsible disclosure program.
